turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Visiting a Medical Clinic and Booking or Changing an Appointment

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Hi, excuse me. I want to see a doctor.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Of course! Absolutely, you’ve come to the right place. Let me just help you with that. So you’re looking to book an appointment with one of our doctors today or another day?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Uh, yes. New one.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Perfect, no problem at all — we see new patients all the time. Before I look at the schedule, I’ll just ask a couple of quick questions so I can book you in properly. What seems to be going on?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Stomach. Something strange.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Okay, stomach issues — thank you for telling me. When you say “something strange”, are you feeling pain, discomfort, bloating, or maybe a burning feeling?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Bloating. And sometimes burning.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Got it — bloating and sometimes a burning sensation. That’s really helpful information. And has that been happening for a few days, or longer than that?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Two days. Maybe three.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Okay, two to three days — that gives us a good idea. Now let me just check what appointments we have available. Alright… just a moment… okay.

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Okay.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: So today we’re actually fully booked, unfortunately. But the good news is we do have availability tomorrow, and we can usually find something that works around work schedules as well.

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Tomorrow is okay.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Great. So tomorrow we have a couple of options. The first one is in the morning at am, and the second one is later at am. Let me know which one sounds better for you.

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: … No. I work at ten.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Ah yes, that would be cutting it very close — you’re right to flag that. In that case, the am appointment would be much more comfortable and less stressful.

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Yes. .

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Perfect. I’ll lock that in for you. Now I’ll just grab a few details to complete the booking. Can I take your full name, please?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: James Lee.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Lovely, thank you. And just to double-check the spelling — that’s J-A-M-E-S, L-E-E, is that right?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Yes.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Great, all sorted. Now, do you have Medicare, or are you covered by Overseas Student Health Cover?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Student insurance. OSHC.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Perfect — OSHC is absolutely fine here. Just make sure you bring your card with you tomorrow so we can process everything smoothly.

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Okay.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: And just so you know what to expect, you’ll pay for the appointment after you see the doctor, and then you can submit a claim to your insurance provider if you need to.

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Claim later?

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Yes, exactly — pay first, then claim afterward. A lot of students do it that way.

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Okay.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: We also ask patients to arrive about ten minutes early. That just gives you time to fill out a short paper form at reception — nothing complicated, just basic details.

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Paper is fine.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Great, that makes things easy. Now, just one more thing — during the appointment, you can absolutely ask the doctor about medication, tests, or anything else you’re worried about.

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Test also? Like blood?

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Yes, if the doctor thinks it’s necessary, they can arrange blood tests or other checks. And if you need a medical certificate for work, you can request that as well.

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Yes. Medical certificate.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: No problem at all — very common request. Alright, let me just recap everything so we’re on the same page. You’re booked in for tomorrow at am. Please bring your OSHC card, arrive about ten minutes early, and let the doctor know about the bloating and burning after meals.

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Tomorrow. . Okay.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Perfect. You’re all set. If anything changes or your symptoms get worse tonight, don’t hesitate to seek urgent care. Otherwise, we’ll see you tomorrow.

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Okay. Thank you.
